text: Testing plays a vital role in software development. Unit tests check small parts of code, like a single function, to ensure they work correctly. Integration tests verify that different parts of the application work together as expected. Many developers use test-driven development (TDD), where they write tests before writing the actual code. This approach guides the development process and helps catch issues early. Overall, thorough testing gives developers greater confidence. They can refactor code or add new features without worrying about breaking existing functionality. This leads to more reliable software and saves time in the long run.
